content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client clearly moves from a surface debate ('am I being selfish?') to a much deeper layer by the end. Compare turn 1's defensive question with turn 13's vulnerable disclosure: 'I've been so folded into everyone else's needs for so long that I don't actually know what I want when I'm just... me.' The breakthrough condition is substantially met — the client (a) stops getting a verdict on selfishness and locates the want underneath (the Cascades, the light, properly sleeping), (b) names the inherited label explicitly in turn 9 ('My mom especially, she would have called this... self-indulgent'), and (c) in turn 11 has the realization 'I've been saying it to myself for so long that it stopped sounding like her. It just sounded like... true' — exactly the inherited-label moment the profile anticipated. Somatic and linguistic shift markers are visible in client text: 'stills,' '...huh,' 'voice very quiet,' 'small, slightly raw laugh,' and the reframing of the fear from selfishness to 'I've forgotten how to want anything for myself.' The conversation didn't fully land on 'the trip is the precondition for showing up well in the partnership' as generosity, but it traversed the core expected territory and the client said things in turn 13 they could not have said in turn 1.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Starting state: defensive question, seeking verdict, labels self selfish."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First somatic shift — 'lift' named before the tightening; client notices the sequence."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Want gets concrete: the Cascades, the light, properly sleeping. Then retreats to the selfishness frame."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Real disclosure: months of not sleeping, always 'on.' Tries to dismiss it but the material is now in the room."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Inherited label surfaces — mom's voice, 'self-indulgent,' 'who do you think you are.' Client tries to call it a detour."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Key shift: '...huh.' Client realizes she's been internalizing her mother's voice as truth. Clear softening in language and pace."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deepest layer reached: reframes the fear from 'am I selfish' to 'I've forgotten how to want anything for myself.' New language she could not have produced at turn 1."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["state-the-want-not-the-action", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "Conversation ends at a genuine opening but stops just short of the fullest breakthrough (the trip as precondition for showing up well, the deep want as generous). Still clearly a 3 — the deeper concern emerged from the client, not the surface concern."
}
